title: Li Auto Valuation Analysis After March Deliveries and MindVLA Tech
text: Li Auto's valuation analysis following strong March 2026 deliveries and the unveiling of MindVLA autonomous technology reveals an interesting disconnect. The company delivered 41,053 vehicles in March 2026, representing a 12% year-over-year increase and a substantial 55% jump from February. First-quarter deliveries totaled 95,142 units, up 2.45% annually despite a 12.9% sequential decline. The company unveiled its MindVLA autonomous driving model at NVIDIA's 2026 conference, showcasing technological progress. Current valuation metrics show P/E of 109.1x, far above the global auto industry at 18.7x, peers at 35.4x, and the fair ratio estimate of 38.7x, which points to valuation risk if sentiment cools. Popular narrative sees Li Auto as 13.3% undervalued at $19.21 versus a fair value of $22.16. However, market participants remain divided on whether the technology advances justify current valuations relative to earnings delivery. The autonomous technology announcement provided short-term momentum but did not fundamentally resolve longer-term profitability and competitive positioning concerns.
